violations.description: General employee practices. — --Food employee observed without proper hair restraint. All line cooks/food prep employees need to wear hair restraints.
Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution between uses.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Nonfood contact equipment/utensil * was not maintained in a good repair or sound condition. Seals on 2-door prep cooler need repaired or replaced.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Microwaves Inside 2-door prep cooler cutting board on prep cooler--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. All door handles on all coolers/Freezers--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. Venthood over flattop needs cleaned.
Toilet facilities. — --Women's Toilet room was not provided with sufficient mechanical ventilation. Both Vents in women's and men's bathrooms need cleaned of Debris.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…)* were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean.--Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…)* were not maintained in good repair. Ceiling tiles need repaired, some are missing.
Lighting. — --Lighting did not have protective shielding in areas where there is exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, or unwrapped single-service items * In cooking area.

Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. Mops and brooms need to be hung up in a way that provides for them to air dry when not being used.
General employee cleanliness. — --Observed employee eating food w/ gloves on, did not changes gloves and wash hands when returning to work.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food must be protected from contamination until use. - Boxed beverages and freezer items were stored on the floor. - Remove potting soil from storage in backroom.
Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Cooked meats and raw meats cannot be stored on the same shelf.

Cooling of potentially hazardous food. — --Prime rib located in walk in cooler from 9/3/25 measured at 49 F. - Hot foods must be cooled from 140F. to 70 F within 2 hours. --loosely covered, ice bath, put in shallow metal pans. - Must be cooled from 70f. to 41 or below within 4 hours.
General equipment and utensils - Use limitations of certain materials. — --Prep cooler in cook line: door has exposed insulation held together by duct tape.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Interior of prep cooler drawers must be cleaned of food debris and mildew.
Plumbing system. — --Fix water leak in the utility/storage room.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Replace/repair floor tiles throughout the kitchen and storage rooms. Should have no missing tiles. - Clean walls around mop sink. - Clean floors, ceilings as needed.
Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Hang mops so they can properly air dry. Currently laying in mop sink.
Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Insecticides must be stored separately from other cleaners. PT alpine fly bait + Keystone aerosol spray not approved in food establishments.
